title: AST SpaceMobile Expands US Manufacturing with Texas and Florida Facilities
text: AST SpaceMobile expanded its total manufacturing footprint to 500,000 square feet. Texas is the company's primary manufacturing hub with five facilities, including a new site in Midland, where AST SpaceMobile builds its BlueBird satellites from raw materials to finished spacecraft. The company also has a location in Maryland and a newly added facility in Homestead, Florida. The Midland facility's production capacity is six satellites per month, translating to roughly 72 satellites annually. The company now employs more than 1,800 professionals, the majority based at its West Texas headquarters. AST SpaceMobile reports 95 percent of production processes are vertically integrated and maintained under U.S. control. The manufacturing expansion reflects the company's effort to scale production of its next-generation BlueBird satellites for its space-based cellular broadband network. This vertical integration and domestic manufacturing positioning strengthen ASTS's supply chain resilience and national security credentials.
